inspectedSource.title: The Ascension of Isaiah
inspectedSource.witness: R.H. Charles text as hosted by Early Christian Writings

decision.summary: The inspected Early Christian Writings witness confirms that the repo candidate is not a safe local chapter-split repair. The source has chapter 3 continuing through the Beliar/Beloved material that the repo candidate separated into chapter 4, and then has a distinct source chapter 4 beginning with the completion-of-the-world/Beliar ruler section. Because the repo candidate also differs in earlier chapter wording and verse shape, it must be rebuilt from a recorded source extraction before collation rather than repaired by moving only one local chapter boundary.

sourceShapeEvidence.evidence: Source chapter 3 verse 13 begins: For Beliar was in great wrath against Isaiah by reason of the vision...
sourceShapeEvidence.repoImplication: This is the material the repo blocker had identified as the beginning of repo chapter 4. It belongs inside source chapter 3 in the inspected witness.

sourceShapeEvidence.evidence: Source chapter 4 begins: AND now Hezekiah and Josab my son, these are the days of the completion of the world.
sourceShapeEvidence.repoImplication: The inspected witness contains a distinct source chapter 4 not represented by the current repo chapter 4 shape.

sourceShapeEvidence.evidence: Source chapter 11 concludes with: Here endeth the vision of Isaiah the prophet with his ascension.
sourceShapeEvidence.repoImplication: The inspected witness is complete through the closing chapter.

nextAllowedWork: extract the complete Early Christian Writings Charles source text into a recorded local snapshot using a bounded connector-safe or manually reviewed extraction
rebuild the candidate from that source snapshot in source chapter shape
only after rebuild and QC perform row-level source binding/collation
